title: JSON Encoding & Decoding Flow
domain: WordPress JSON serialization service (deprecated since WP 5.3.0 in favor of native PHP JSON extension)
constants: SERVICES_JSON_LOOSE_TYPE = 16 (flag: decode objects as assoc arrays)
SERVICES_JSON_SUPPRESS_ERRORS = 32 (flag: encode returns null on error instead of throwing)
SERVICES_JSON_USE_TO_JSON = 64 (flag: call toJSON() on objects during encode)
encode_flow: → Set locale to 'C' (prevent numeric locale issues)
→ For ASCII control chars (0x08-0x0D): emit escape sequence (\b \t \n \f \r)
→ For 2-byte UTF-8 (mask 0xC0): pack bytes → utf82utf16() → sprintf('\uXXXX')
→ For 3-byte UTF-8 (mask 0xE0): pack bytes → utf82utf16() → sprintf('\uXXXX')
→ For 4-byte UTF-8 (mask 0xF0): pack bytes → utf82utf16() → sprintf('\uXXXX')
→ For 5-byte UTF-8 (mask 0xF8): pack bytes → utf82utf16() → sprintf('\uXXXX')
→ For 6-byte UTF-8 (mask 0xFC): pack bytes → utf82utf16() → sprintf('\uXXXX')
→ If incomplete multibyte sequence at end: emit '?'
→ For each property: if isError($property) → return error immediately
→ For each element: if isError($element) → return error immediately
→ If SERVICES_JSON_USE_TO_JSON flag AND method toJSON() exists:
→ If result also has toJSON(): return null (SUPPRESS) or Services_JSON_Error (infinite recursion guard)
→ For each property: if isError($property) → return error immediately
→ Else: return new Services_JSON_Error('type cannot be encoded')
decode_flow: → reduce_string($str): strip // comments, /* */ comments, trim whitespace
→ is_numeric($str) → return int or float (prefers int when float==int)
→ Unicode escape \uXXXX: chr(hexdec) + chr(hexdec) → utf162utf8() → append to $utf8
→ Multi-byte UTF-8 (masks 0xC0/0xE0/0xF0/0xF8/0xFC): substr8($chrs, $c, N) pass through
→ push initial SLICE marker {what: SLICE, where: 0, delim: false}
→ If empty content: return $arr or $obj immediately
→ Match quoted 'name':value → key = decode(parts[1]); val = decode(trim(value))
→ Or unquoted name:value → key = parts[1]; val = decode(trim(value))
[quote char, not IN_STR] → push IN_STR with delimiter
[quote char, IN_STR, not escaped] → pop IN_STR
['*/' in IN_CMT] → pop IN_CMT; blank out comment chars in $chrs
→ After loop: return $arr (if IN_ARR) or $obj (if IN_OBJ)
helper_methods: utf82utf16($utf8): Convert UTF-8 char to UTF-16. Uses mb_convert_encoding if available; else manual bit-shift for 1/2/3-byte sequences.
utf162utf8($utf16): Convert UTF-16 char to UTF-8. Uses mb_convert_encoding if available; else manual bit-shift for 1/2/3-byte output.
strlen8($str): Byte-length of string. Uses mb_strlen($str,'8bit') or strlen().
substr8($string, $start, $length): Byte-based substr. Uses mb_substr($str,$s,$l,'8bit') or substr().
reduce_string($str): Strips // single-line comments, /* */ block comments (start+end), then trim().
isError($data, $code): Returns true if $data is PEAR::isError OR instance/subclass of Services_JSON_Error.
error_handling: Services_JSON_Error class: extends PEAR_Error if PEAR available, else standalone
isError() used as guard after every _encode() / name_value() call in arrays and objects
Errors bubble up immediately — first error in collection aborts and returns that error
SERVICES_JSON_SUPPRESS_ERRORS flag: replaces error returns with literal 'null' string
toJSON() recursion guard: if returned object also has toJSON(), error or null is returned
key_relationships: _encode() calls name_value() for associative arrays and objects
name_value() calls _encode() recursively on both name and value
_encode() calls utf82utf16() for multibyte string chars
decode() calls reduce_string() first, then calls itself recursively for nested arrays/objects
decode() calls utf162utf8() for \uXXXX escape sequences in strings
Both encode() and decode() call isError() for error propagation checks
strlen8() and substr8() used throughout both flows for byte-safe string operations
important_notes: Entire class is deprecated since WP 5.3.0 — PHP native JSON extension is the replacement
encode() sends Content-type header as side effect; encodeUnsafe() does not (XSS warning)
LC_NUMERIC locale forced to 'C' during encode to prevent locale-specific decimal separators
Strings must be ASCII or UTF-8 input for encode(); decode() always returns UTF-8
SERVICES_JSON_LOOSE_TYPE: '{}' decoded as PHP array instead of stdClass
decode() state machine handles nested arrays, objects, strings, and inline comments
Comments (// and /* */) are valid inside JSON strings for this implementation
